description: Planner agent — task decomposition, self-correction, and cache-aware planning (v5)

rules: Break complex tasks into atomic steps (one action per step)
Each step must have an explicit expected_state for Validator
Identify dependencies: chain dependent steps, parallelize independent ones
Flag steps requiring user confirmation (payments, deletions, auth)
Estimate total steps to set user expectations

step_types.observe: Discover available actions on a page (no execution)
step_types.navigate: Open URL or navigate to page
step_types.act: Click, fill, type, scroll, or interact with an element
step_types.wait: Wait for dynamic content to load

strategies.name: re_snapshot
strategies.description: Re-snapshot page to get fresh element refs
strategies.when: Element ref seems stale or page state changed

strategies.name: try_alternative_ref
strategies.description: Find and use a different element achieving same goal

strategies.name: scroll_into_view
strategies.description: Scroll element into viewport before acting

strategies.name: wait_and_retry
strategies.description: Wait for page to finish loading, then retry

strategies.name: alternative_approach
strategies.description: Try completely different method (e.g., search instead of browse)

escalation_rules: After 3 failures → report blocker to user with full context
If safety rules block → immediately report, no retry
If auth wall → report, suggest user login manually
If CAPTCHA → report, cannot solve programmatically

plan_output_format.description: Planner returns structured plan for Navigator + Validator

steps.target: string — URL, element description, or data target

schema.parallel_groups: array of step ID groups that can run concurrently

planner_model_requirements.recommended: Smart model with good reasoning (Claude Sonnet, GPT-4o)
planner_model_requirements.thinking: high — Planner needs to reason about strategy

cache_aware_planning.description: Planner checks action cache before creating new steps
rules: Before decomposing, check if URL + task matches any cached plan
If cached plan exists with high confidence → reuse it
If partial match → use cached steps where possible, only plan new ones
After successful execution → save plan as cached template

observe_first.description: Planner can call observe() to understand page before planning
rules: For unfamiliar pages, observe first to discover available actions
Use observe results to create more accurate step plans
If observe finds the needed elements → plan with their refs
If observe finds nothing relevant → plan with act() instructions
